topic.name: Transportation Infrastructure
topic.description: The documents discuss various transportation infrastructures, including railroads and highways, across different regions in the United States and Canada.

Document 1: The Dakota, Minnesota and Eastern Railroad is a Class II railroad subsidiary of the Canadian Pacific Railway operating across South Dakota and southern Minnesota.

Document 2: Maryland Route 213 is a 68.25-mile state highway located on the Eastern Shore of Maryland.

topic.name: Naval Ships and Their Histories
topic.description: This topic encompasses the design, construction, service, and fate of various naval ships throughout history, particularly focusing on their roles in military conflicts.

Document 1: HMS Campania was a seaplane tender and aircraft carrier, converted from an elderly ocean liner by the Royal Navy early in the First World War.
She never saw combat and was soon relegated to a training role because of her elderly machinery.
Document 2: The Russian ironclad Sevastopol was ordered as a 58-gun wooden frigate by the Imperial Russian Navy in the early 1860s, but was converted while under construction into a 32-gun armored frigate.
Sevastopol was reclassified as a training ship in 1880.
Document 3: SMS Frithjof was the third vessel of the six-member Siegfried class of coastal defense ships built for the German Imperial Navy.
She served in the German fleet throughout the 1890s and was rebuilt in 1900 - 1902.
Document 4: The Warrior-class cruiser was a class of four armoured cruisers built for the Royal Navy in the first decade of the 20th century.
After commissioning, all four sister ships were assigned to the Channel and Home Fleets until 1913.
Document 5: The Meteor class was a pair of two avisos built by the Imperial German Navy in the late-1880s.
They served less than two years on active duty, from 1893 to 1895.
Document 6: Mesudiye was a central-battery ironclad of the Ottoman Navy, one of the largest ships of that type ever built.
The ship saw extensive action during the First Balkan War in 1912 – 13.
Document 7: SMS Siegfried was the lead ship of the six-member Siegfried class of coastal defense ships built for the German Imperial Navy.
She was ultimately broken up for scrap in 1920.
Document 8: HMS Bulldog was a B-class destroyer built for the Royal Navy from 1929 to 1931.
She saw service throughout World War II on convoy escort duty during the Battle of the Atlantic.
Document 9: The Action of 6 November 1794 was a naval engagement during the French Revolutionary Wars.
The battle was a rare French victory, lying between the significant British victories at the Glorious First of June and the Battle of Groix.
Document 10: The Admiral Spiridov class were a pair of monitors built for the Imperial Russian Navy in the late 1860s.
They were reclassified as coast-defense ironclads in 1892 before they became training ships in 1900.

topic.name: Animal Biology and Ecology
topic.description: This topic encompasses the study of various animal species, their behaviors, adaptations, and ecological roles within their environments.

1: Entoprocta, whose name means 'anus inside', is a phylum of mostly sessile aquatic animals.

10: Ecology is the scientific analysis and study of interactions among organisms and their environment.
Topics of interest to ecologists include the diversity, distribution, amount (biomass), and number (population) of particular organisms.

topic.name: Historical Atlantic Hurricanes
topic.description: This topic encompasses the historical accounts and impacts of various hurricanes that have occurred in the Atlantic Ocean, detailing their formation, intensity, landfalls, and resulting damages.

Document 1: Hurricane Brenda of August 1973 was the first tropical cyclone on record to make landfall in the Mexican Province of Campeche.
A total of 10 people were killed by the hurricane and over 2,000 more were left homeless.
Document 2: Tropical Storm Bret made a rare landfall on the Delmarva Peninsula in June 1981.
Impact in the United States was generally minor.
Document 3: The 1940 Louisiana hurricane caused record flooding across much of the Southern United States in August 1940.
Reports of a potentially destructive hurricane near the United States Gulf Coast forced thousands of residents in low-lying areas to evacuate.
Document 4: The 1884 Atlantic hurricane season was one of only three Atlantic hurricane seasons, along with 1852 and 1858, in which every known tropical cyclone attained hurricane status.
This storm also left damage to crops and vessels in portions of the Bahamas and Cuba.
Document 5: Hurricane Doreen was considered the worst tropical cyclone to affect California in 32 years.
Flooding left 2,000 people homeless in Mexicali along the United States-Mexico border.
Document 6: Hurricane Fred was one of the easternmost forming major hurricanes in the North Atlantic basin since satellite observations became available.
The storm degenerated into a remnant-low later that day.
Document 7: Hurricane Emily was the only major hurricane to develop during the below-average 1987 Atlantic hurricane season.
The storm caused $50 million in damages and injured 16 people.
Document 8: Hurricane Klaus was a North Atlantic hurricane that hit the Leeward Islands from the west in November of the 1984 Atlantic hurricane season.
Klaus caused heavy marine damage in the Leeward Islands, including wrecking at least three ships.
Document 9: Hurricane Floyd was the only hurricane to make landfall in the United States in the 1987 Atlantic hurricane season.
It passed through the Bahamas before becoming extratropical and later dissipating.
Document 10: The 1853 Atlantic hurricane season featured eight known tropical cyclones, none of which made landfall.
The strongest cyclone of the season peaked at Category 4 strength with 150 mph (240 km / h) winds.

3: There 's a Riot Goin ' On is the fifth studio album by American band Sly and the Family Stone, released on November 20, 1971.
The album embraced a darker and more challenging sound than the psychedelic soul style of the group's previous records.
4: The ArchAndroid is the second studio album and major label debut by American recording artist Janelle Monáe, released on May 18, 2010.
The album has been compared to the work of artists such as David Bowie, Outkast, Prince and Michael Jackson.
5: The new school of hip hop was a movement in hip hop music starting 1983 – 84 with the early records of Run – D.M.C. and LL Cool J.
New school artists made shorter songs that could more easily gain radio play.
6: Winter in America is a studio album by American vocalist Gil Scott-Heron and keyboardist Brian Jackson, released in May 1974.
The subject matter on Winter in America deals with the African-American community and inner city in the 1970s.
7: Live ! at the Star-Club in Hamburg, Germany; 1962 is a double album featuring live performances by The Beatles, recorded in late December 1962.
The album provides historic insight into the group's club act in the period after Ringo Starr joined but before the emergence of Beatlemania.
8: The Beatles members John Lennon, Paul McCartney, George Harrison, Stuart Sutcliffe and Pete Best regularly performed at different clubs in Hamburg, Northern Germany, during the period from August 1960 to December 1962.
This chapter in the group's history honed their performance skills, widened their reputation, and led to their first recording.
9: The Allman Brothers Band is the debut studio album by American rock band the Allman Brothers Band, released in the United States by Atco Records and Capricorn Records on November 4, 1969.
The album combines blues, jazz and country music to varying degrees.
10: The Concert for Bangladesh was the collective name for two benefit concerts organised by George Harrison and Ravi Shankar.
The event was the first-ever benefit concert of such a magnitude and featured a supergroup of performers.

topic.name: Science Fiction Television Series
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various science fiction television series, their episodes, characters, and narrative arcs.

Document 1: The Pandorica Opens is the twelfth episode in the fifth series of British science fiction television programme Doctor Who
the mysterious character River Song summons alien time traveller the Doctor
Document 2: The second season of the American science fiction television series Fringe commenced airing on the Fox network
the season followed the continuation of a war between two universes
Document 3: The Angels Take Manhattan is the fifth episode of the seventh series of the revived British science fiction television series Doctor Who
alien time traveller the Doctor takes his companions Amy Pond and her husband Rory Williams to Central Park
Document 10: Night Terrors is the ninth episode of the sixth series of the British science fiction television series Doctor Who
the Doctor and his companions decide to make a 'house call' to an eight-year-old boy named George
